what_it_does: Defines the icon design grammar, color roles, starter icon set, SVG fragments, and usage contexts for the counterpart-design icon face.
observations: Meta frames the icon face as a construction grammar rather than a fixed imported set.
Construction rules specify a 24-grid, 1.5 stroke width, round caps and joins, line-only rendering, small-radius corners, and color role tokens.
The set contains ten named icons with inline SVG fragments: house, ledger, plan, chat, calendar, crane, scale, compass, chart, and plug.
Usage contexts are strip, anchor, and node.
Meta says the drawn paths now live in this file and are consumed into a generated DNA:ICONS block elsewhere.
standouts: The file combines design rationale, construction constraints, token references, and drawable SVG fragments in one ledger entry.
The two-tone rule limits active icons to one gold element.
The vocabulary is explicitly described as extensible through grammar rather than imported icon assets.
conventions: Icon entries use an id plus an svg string containing SVG child elements rather than full svg wrappers.
Paths use a 24-unit coordinate space and line-oriented primitives.
Color values are represented as token references rather than literal colors.
The prose uses uppercase emphasis for conceptual terms such as ICON, CONSTRUCTION GRAMMAR, HERE, and CONSTRUCTED.
concerns: SVG fragments are embedded as strings, so structure is not directly typed or separately validated in this JSON file.
The construction rules define an active two-tone behavior, but individual icon entries do not encode active-state variants.
notes: Brief this as the icon grammar and starter SVG vocabulary for the design DNA layer.
questions: How are new constructed icons reviewed against the grammar?
Where is the one-gold-element active state applied if it is not represented in each SVG fragment?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file contents: it documents the icon construction grammar and stores the named starter icon SVG fragments.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to preserve a coherent icon language by pairing explicit drawing constraints with a small reusable vocabulary of inline glyph fragments.
novelty: The file treats icons as a generative design substrate while also storing the actual starter glyph geometry in JSON.
contribution: Contributes a structured icon face: construction rules, semantic icon names, drawable SVG fragments, color-role intent, and placement contexts that a fused system would keep as an icon substrate.
summary_for_embedding: This is a JSON design-DNA ledger defining a 24-grid line-icon grammar, color roles, usage contexts, and ten starter SVG icon fragments.
intention_for_embedding: This was for making the icon layer operational as a coherent, extensible construction system rather than a borrowed fixed icon set.
